world.title: Volleyball tourney organized
world.description: Excelsior Sports Correspondent SAMBA, Mar 28: Samba Police organized a volleyball tournament under the Civic Action Programme (CAP) 2025-2026 at Government Higher Secondary School Kharra Madana, falling under the jurisdiction of Police Station Samba. The tournament witnessed enthusiastic participation from five teams from across the district and drew a large number of local spectators, reflecting strong community engagement and sporting spirit. In the final match, village Madana team clinched the title by defeating village Kharra team in an exciting contest. […]

science.title: Supercomputer simulations map spliceosome motions in a two-million-atom human cell model
science.description: A new study from the Italian Institute of Technology (IIT), in collaboration with Uppsala University (Sweden) and AstraZeneca, shows how computational chemistry and supercomputers can help scientists better understand the fundamental mechanisms of life, specifically those of human cells. This research was conducted by the Molecular Modeling and Drug Discovery Unit, led by Marco De Vivo at IIT in Genoa, and was published in the journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences.

ai.title: Mark Cuban Fires Back At Musk's 'Work Optional' Vision With Mock IPO Risk Filing As SpaceX IPO Looms
ai.description: Mark Cuban outlined AI automation, tax, and market-structure risks faced by companies replacing human workers with humanoids and AI in a mock IPO risk-disclosure post on X on Saturday. Cuban’s post was in response to Elon Musk‘s statement, “Working will be optional in the future,” posted on X.

ai.description: By Suhaib Bakshi bakshisuhaib094@gmail.com Artificial intelligence is rapidly emerging as one of the most consequential technologies shaping the global economy. For India, the central challenge is not only how to develop advanced AI systems but also how to ensure that the technology contributes meaningfully to economic growth, productivity and public services. Much of the global conversation around artificial intelligence has focused on the race to build ever larger frontier models, with major advances coming from countries such as the […]

ai.title: The Wired Belts are the new Rust Belts: Report ranks which jobs are most vulnerable
ai.description: Digital Planet, the research center at the forefront of researching the AI transformation at The Fletcher School at Tufts University, today released the American AI Jobs Risk Index. It is a first-of-its-kind data-driven framework that maps the potential of AI-driven job vulnerability across every major occupation, industry, metropolitan area, and state in the United States. Drawing on 15 years of labor market data and the most current AI adoption research, the Index goes beyond prior studies by measuring actual vulnerability to job loss—not merely exposure—and connecting that vulnerability directly to projected income loss and geography.

ai.title: Gilles Arsenault removed from P.E.I. cabinet
